title: 4 Managing User Access to the File and Report Exchange
body_text:
4	Managing User Access to the File and Report Exchange
4.1	Overview of Managing User Access to the File and Report Exchange
User management for the File and Report Exchange is currently managed through CPS Admin or User Management. See the CPS Admin Application Part for procedures on adding and modifying Client Centre Users and CPS Admin Security Officers. Alternatively, if your financial institution is using User Management, see the User Management Part. CPS Admin Security Officers can also delete and modify other CPS Admin Security Officers via the User Management application, with some exceptions.
Financial institutions and business members can connect to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange via direct connection (SFTP) or via the Client Centre. Those who require access to the File and Report Exchange must log in via the Client Centre.
Note:	If your financial institutionâs business members require access to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange (e.g., to download files for Bill Payment Remittance Processing services or to upload AFT files), you can distribute procedures to your business members as described in Section 1.3, Providing File and Report Exchange Procedures to Business Members.
4.2	User Management â Access via the Client Centre (Financial Institution Users)
Access to the File and Report Exchange for financial institution users is via the Client Centre. Therefore, each user must be set up as either a Client Centre User or a CPS Admin Security Officer, then assigned access to the File and Report Exchange. CPS Admin Security Officers must have administrative access to the File and Report Exchange to create File and Report Exchange users.
See the CPS Admin Application Part for procedures on adding, modifying, and deleting Client Centre Users and CPS Admin Security Officers. Alternatively, see the User Management Part.
The following subsections provide procedures for CPS Admin Security Officers:
Granting File and Report Exchange Access to a Client Centre User
Granting File and Report Exchange Access to an Existing CPS Admin Security Officer
Granting File and Report Exchange Access to a New CPS Admin Security Officer
Granting File and Report Exchange Access to a Client Centre User
There are currently two user management applications running parallel: The Central Portal System (CPS) Admin and User Management. Client Centre Users are displayed in both systems, so you can assign access to the File and Report Exchange in either CPS Admin or User Management.
This subsection describes how a CPS Admin Security Officer assigns a Client Centre User access to the File and Report Exchange via CPS Admin. For information on how to assign File and Report Exchange access via User Management, see the User Management Part.
You must be authorized to assign File and Report Exchange access to others (i.e., you must have administrative access rights) before proceeding. See Subsection, Granting File and Report Exchange Access to an Existing CPS Admin Security Officer.
To grant File and Report Exchange access to a new or existing Client Centre User:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select Sign In. See Figure 1.
Note: 	Youâll be prompted to enter your username before this page appears if itâs your first login to the Client Centre, youâve deleted your cookies, or youâre using incognito/private browsing.
Figure 1: Client Centre Login
Select
Applications
. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Select
CPS Admin
. See Figure 3.
Note: 	Applications are grouped by type (e.g., Admin & Support, Payments). If needed, scroll down the page to find the application. If youâve saved the application as a favourite, it will also appear under âMy Favouritesâ.
Figure 3: Accessing CPS Admin
Enter the code generated on your registered 2-step security token, then select
Done
. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: 2-Step Security Code
Select
Click here
. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: CPS Administration Access
Select
Edit User
to add File and Report Exchange to an existing Client Centre User. See the CPS Admin Application Part for procedures on editing Client Centre Users.
Alternatively, select
Add User
if the individual isnât yet set up as a Client Centre User. See the CPS Admin Application Part for procedures on creating Client Centre Users.
Scroll down to the Applications Menu once the userâs profile displays, then check the box beside âFile-and-Report-Exchangeâ. To assign the user access to a specific folder on the File and Report Exchange,
enter âYâ
in the applicable text fields. If the user doesnât require access to a folder, leave the text field blank. To modify an existing userâs access to specific folders, add or remove the âYâ as necessary. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Assigning Folder Access
Select
Submit
at the bottom of the page.
Have another CPS Admin Security Officer authorize the task.
Complete the FTP User Request, Form 2010, and submit it as directed on page 2 of the form.
Granting File and Report Exchange Access to an Existing CPS Admin Security Officer
There are two ways to manage your CPS Admin Security Officers, depending on the change required. You can either send a request to Central 1 to make a change on your behalf, or you can make the change directly on the User Management application.
If your financial institution has at least two existing CPS Admin Security Officers with administrative access to the File and Report Exchange, they can assign other CPS Admin Security Officers with personal and/or administrative access to the File and Report Exchange via User Management. See the User Management Part for more information. Otherwise, complete the CPS Admin Modification/Deletion Request, Form 2035, as described below.
To grant File and Report Exchange access to an existing CPS Admin Security Officer via Central 1:
Complete the CPS Admin Modification/Deletion Request, Form 2035. See Figure 7. Completion of the form is self-explanatory, but note the following:
â
Select
Add
.
â¡
Change Request Details â Personal Access Rights
â
Record âFile-and-Report-Exchangeâ to give the individual access to the File and Report Exchange.
â
List the folders to which the individual requires access.
â¢
Change Request Details â Administrative Rights
â
Record âFile-and-Report-Exchangeâ if the individual is authorized to assign File and Report Exchange access to Client Centre Users.
â
Record âCredit Union Client Files and Reportsâ if the individual is authorized to grant File-and-Report-Exchange access to business members
â£
Financial Institution Authorization
â
Ensure that two authorized signing officers sign the form.
Figure 7: CPS Admin Modification/Deletion Request, Form 2035
Forward the form to your central credit union as indicated on page 2. If applicable, the central credit union will authorize the form and forward it to Central 1.
Retain a copy of the form for your records.
Complete the FTP User Request, Form 2010, and submit it as directed on page 2 of the form.
Granting File and Report Exchange Access to a New CPS Admin Security Officer
CPS Admin Security Officers canât create other CPS Admin Security Officers. These requests must be sent to Central 1. To grant File and Report Exchange access to a new CPS Admin Security Officer:
Complete the CPS Admin Appointment Certificate, Form 1918. See Figure 8. Completion of the form is self-explanatory, but note the following:
â
Personal Access Rights
â
Record File-and-Report-Exchange to give the individual access to the File and Report Exchange.
â
List the folders to which the individual requires access.
â¡
Administrative Rights
â
Record âFile-and-Report-Exchangeâ if the individual is authorized to assign File and Report Exchange access to Client Centre Users and other CPS Admin Security Officers (personal access only).
â
Record âCredit Union Client Files and Reportsâ if the individual is authorized to grant File-and-Report-Exchange access to business members
â¢
Financial Institution Authorization
â
Ensure that two authorized signing officers sign the form.
Figure 8: CPS Admin Appointment Certificate, Form 1918
Forward the form to your central credit union as indicated on page 2. If applicable, the central credit union will authorize the form and forward it to Central 1.
Retain a copy of the form for your records.
Central 1 will communicate a unique one-time password to the new CPS Admin Security Officer. See the CPS Admin Application Part for procedures on registering a Client Centre password.
Have another CPS Admin Security Officer assign a 2-step security token to the new CPS Admin Security Officer. See the 2-Step Security Part for procedures.
Complete the FTP User Request, Form 2010, and submit it as directed on page 2 of the form.
Related Information
CPS Admin Appointment Certificate,
Form 1918
CPS Admin Modification/Deletion Request,
Form 2035
4.3	User Management â Access via the Client Centre (Business Member Users)
Business member users who access the File and Report Exchange via the Client Centre must be set up as Client Centre Users and assigned access to the File and Report Exchange. CPS Admin Security Officers at your financial institution manage business member users via CPS Admin or User Management. CPS Admin Security Officers must have administrative access to the File and Report Exchange to create File and Report Exchange users.
Note:	Although business member users are authenticated on the Client Centre, they use a different URL than financial institution users. Business member users go directly to the File and Report Exchange application because there is content on the Client Centre landing page that isnât applicable to them.
Granting File and Report Exchange Access to a Business Member User
There are currently two user management applications running parallel: The Central Portal System (CPS) Admin and User Management. Client Centre Users are displayed in both systems, so you can assign access to the File and Report Exchange in either CPS Admin or User Management.
This section describes how a CPS Admin Security Officer assigns a business member user access to the File and Report Exchange via CPS Admin. For information on how to assign File and Report Exchange access via User Management, see the User Management Part. You must be authorized to assign File and Report Exchange access to others (i.e., you must have administrative access rights) before proceeding.
To grant File and Report Exchange access to a business member user:
Create a profile for the business member user on CPS Admin as you would a financial institution user. See Section 4.2, User Management â Access via the Client Centre (Financial Institution Users).
Scroll down to the Applications Menu and select
Credit-Union-Client-Files-and-Reports
. This will allow the business member user to view and upload or download files. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: Assigning Application Access
Complete the FTP User Request, Form 2010. Completion of this form is self-explanatory. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: FTP User Request, Form 2010
Forward the form as directed on page 2 of the form.
Central 1 will notify your financial institution when the new business member user is set up. You can then advise the business member user to log in. Since additional set up is required at Central 1, the business member user should wait for confirmation before attempting to log in to the File and Report Exchange.
Provide new business member users with procedures on accessing and using the File and Report Exchange. See Section 1.3, Providing File and Report Exchange Procedures to Business Members.
Note:	Delete a business member user on CPS Admin as you would a financial institution user. See the CPS Admin Application Part or the User Management Part for procedures.
Related Information
FTP User Request,
Form 2010
4.4	User Management â Access via Direct Connection (SFTP) (All Users)
The procedures in this section apply to all financial institution and business member users who request a direct connection (SFTP) to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange.
A direct connection (SFTP) is required if scripts are used to exchange files. Central 1 does not provide support for scripts or the FTP software used to connect to the File and Report Exchange, but it does support the connection. Contact the FTP software manufacturer for FTP software related questions.
To add, modify, or delete users who access the File and Report Exchange via direct connection (SFTP):
Complete the FTP User Request, Form 2010. Completion of this form is self-explanatory. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: FTP User Request, Form 2010
Forward the form as directed on page 2 of the form.
Central 1 will provide the user with login credentials and the technical information required to access the File and Report Exchange via direct connection (SFTP).
If the new user is for a business member, provide the user with procedures on accessing the File and Report Exchange. See Section 1.3, Providing File and Report Exchange Procedures to Business Members.
Related Information
FTP User Request,
Form 2010
END
